what_it_does: This file is an operations runbook that records diagnosed failure modes for the substrate setup, pairing each symptom with its underlying cause and the fix that resolves it.
observations: Entries follow a symptom → cause → fix structure.
Covers service-start failures, inference-output problems, and VRAM/RAM issues.
Includes concrete commands like journalctl, nvidia-smi, dmesg, and ps.
References linked guides such as cuda-toolchain, chat-template-patch, and systemd-services.
Has a placeholder section for symptom families not yet documented.
Ends with an 'Open at this topic' section noting the list is incomplete by design and suggesting a future diagnostic CLI.
Mentions WSL, systemd user services, vLLM 0.21, and specific model/tool-call parser configurations.
standouts: Explicit goal that future agents should not re-derive fixes — the cost of diagnosis is paid once and recorded.
Cold-start diagnosis distinguishes systemd 'active' status from actual endpoint readiness.
Specific parser mismatch guidance for Qwen3 XML tool calls.
OOM-kill diagnosis using dmesg and journalctl auto-restart evidence.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with type, title, date_started, and tags.
Uses wiki-style internal links in [[...]] notation.
Organizes content with Markdown headings, tables, and fenced code blocks.
Includes 'Purpose', 'Open at this topic', and 'Connects to' sections.
Labels common causes 'in order of frequency observed'.
concerns: The list is intentionally incomplete, so coverage gaps are expected but not enumerated.
Many fixes depend on linked files that are not shown here.
Version-specific advice (vLLM 0.21, cu13 packages) may become stale.
No dates or verification records attached to individual entries.
Some recommendations are environment-specific (WSL, user systemd) without stating prerequisites.
notes: A practical, symptom-indexed troubleshooting ledger for vLLM/CUDA/RAM failures in the foundation operations substrate.
questions: What is the exact runtime environment — WSL version, CUDA toolkit version, and vLLM release?
Are the linked files (cuda-toolchain, chat-template-patch, systemd-services, etc.) maintained and current?
How are new entries validated before being added?
What services and models are in scope beyond vllm-chat and jina-v4?
Is the proposed vllm-stack diagnose CLI planned or already partially implemented?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it records diagnosed failures with causes and fixes for future reference.
intention_signals: The file tries to turn one-off debugging effort into reusable institutional memory, making substrate problems cheap to resolve for future maintainers or agents.
novelty: It frames troubleshooting as a deliberately incomplete, growing ledger whose value comes from avoiding repeated diagnosis, and it explicitly invites future agents to extend it with the same shape.
contribution: A structured, symptom-indexed record of commands, environmental quirks, and fixes for the vLLM/CUDA service stack that a fused system could keep as a living operational runbook.
summary_for_embedding: A markdown operations runbook documenting diagnosed vLLM service-start, inference-output, and memory failure modes with symptom-cause-fix entries, commands, and links to related substrate guides.
intention_for_embedding: To record once-resolved troubleshooting knowledge so future agents can quickly diagnose and fix common substrate failures without re-deriving them.
